name: Pitney Bowes Employees
description_short: NCUA-insured federal credit union serving Pitney Bowes employees and their families since 1936, offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans from a Shelton, CT headquarters.
description_long:
Pitney Bowes Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 as an employee-sponsored financial institution. It operates as a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union with headquarters at 27 Waterview Drive in Shelton, Connecticut. The institution has grown to hold approximately $59.61 million in assets, serving a membership restricted to Pitney Bowes employees and their immediate family members. This membership limitation reflects its original purpose as a workplace credit union designed to serve a specific employer's workforce.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) ranging from 1 to 5 years, mortgages for both home purchase and refinancing, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), and various loan products. Published rates include 4.20% APY on savings accounts, 4.59% on 1-year CDs, 4.00% on 2 and 5-year CDs, 4.91% on 30-year mortgages, 4.39% on 15-year mortgages, and 0.99% on HELOCs. The institution maintains $47.68 million in deposits, $12.04 million in loans, and $11.56 million in capital.

As an employee credit union, Pitney Bowes Employees distinguishes itself through its exclusive membership model and potential member alignment with a specific employer community. The organization maintains a local physical presence in Connecticut while offering traditional credit union benefits including NCUA insurance protection and federally chartered status. However, the credit union's restricted field of membership significantly limits its accessibility compared to community credit unions or banks.

Prospective members should note that membership eligibility is the primary constraint—only current Pitney Bowes employees and their immediate family qualify for accounts. The website indicates that current deposit and loan rates are not fully updated as of April 2026, suggesting potential challenges in obtaining real-time rate information. Comparison with larger, open-membership credit unions and national banks may be necessary to evaluate competitive positioning on rates and product offerings. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
Established 1936 institution with 90+ years of operational history
Competitive CD rates: 4.59% for 1-year CDs as of 2026
Multiple deposit products including savings and CDs up to 5 years
cons: Membership restricted to Pitney Bowes employees and immediate family only—no open membership
Modest asset base ($59.61M) limits product innovation and branch network expansion
Website indicates deposit and loan rates not currently updated for April 2026
No indication of online banking capabilities or digital service offerings
Limited branch network compared to community credit unions and regional banks
best_for: Current and former Pitney Bowes employees seeking employer-aligned financial services
Immediate family members of Pitney Bowes employees eligible for credit union membership
Connecticut residents in the Shelton area seeking local credit union relationship
Members prioritizing NCUA federal insurance and credit union ownership structure
diagnosis: Pitney Bowes Employees Federal Credit Union is best suited for current and former Pitney Bowes employees and their immediate family members seeking traditional credit union banking services with federal insurance protection. The primary caveat is restrictive membership eligibility—this institution is not accessible to the general public and serves exclusively as an employee credit union, making it suitable only for those with direct employment connection to Pitney Bowes.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
